title: fumana viscida subsp. hispidula
intro: Fumana viscida subsp. hispidula, also known as Rock Whitlow-grass or Fumana, is a plant species belonging to the Cistaceae family. Traditionally, Fumana viscida subsp. hispidula has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Fumana viscida subsp. hispidula can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, and its extracts are often used in skincare products and ointments.
